components.description: Shared Inlay namespace + a designer-friendly spring token and a one-line UIViewPropertyAnimator wrapper. Installed once; many components depend on it.
components.name: spring-animator
components.title: Spring Animator

components.description: A draggable bottom sheet with medium/large/custom detents, rubber-banding, a grabber, an interpolating backdrop dim, and swipe-to-dismiss. Self-contained — presents over any view.
components.name: bottom-sheet
components.title: Bottom Sheet

components.description: A playful buffering indicator — a row of dots with a larger ball bouncing above them. Auto-starts on screen and pauses when off-screen. Zero dependencies.
components.name: bouncing-loader
components.title: Bouncing Loader
components.usage:
let loader = BouncingLoader()
view.addSubview(loader)
loader.translatesAutoresizingMaskIntoConstraints = false
NSLayoutConstraint.activate([
    loader.centerXAnchor.constraint(equalTo: view.centerXAnchor),
    loader.centerYAnchor.constraint(equalTo: view.centerYAnchor),
])

components.description: A celebratory particle overlay (rectangles, circles, triangles, or your image) with rain, fountain, and radial-burst modes, spin, gravity, and a one-shot burst(). Touch-transparent. Zero dependencies.
components.name: confetti-burst
components.title: Confetti Burst

variants.description: Radial blast from a point. The default for celebrations.
variants.title: Burst

components.description: A card that springs from a compact summary to a detailed view, crossfading content with a rotating chevron and an optional background scrim. Sizes correctly in stacks and tables.
components.name: expandable-card
components.title: Expandable Card

components.description: A floating action button that expands into staggered mini-actions with labels, a rotating main icon, and an optional backdrop dim. Expands in any direction.
components.name: fab-speed-dial
components.title: FAB Speed Dial
components.usage:
let fab = SpeedDialButton(mainIcon: UIImage(systemName: "plus"), actions: [
    .init(icon: UIImage(systemName: "camera"), title: "Photo") { },
    .init(icon: UIImage(systemName: "doc"), title: "File") { },
])
view.addSubview(fab)

components.description: A floating, pill-shaped toolbar with a glass or solid background, spring entrance animation, tactile press feedback, and an optional selection highlight.
components.name: floating-toolbar
components.title: Floating Toolbar
components.usage:
let toolbar = FloatingToolbar(items: [
    .init(icon: UIImage(systemName: "house.fill"))      { print("home") },
    .init(icon: UIImage(systemName: "magnifyingglass")) { print("search") },
])
view.addSubview(toolbar)
NSLayoutConstraint.activate([
    toolbar.centerXAnchor.constraint(equalTo: view.centerXAnchor),
    toolbar.bottomAnchor.constraint(equalTo: view.safeAreaLayoutGuide.bottomAnchor, constant: -16),
])

variants.description: Opaque background that adapts to light/dark mode.
variants.title: Solid

components.description: A custom bottom tab bar for 3-5 tabs with a springy sliding indicator (pill, underline, or dot) and an icon that lifts and tints on selection. Emits selection; you swap content.
components.name: home-tab-bar
components.title: Home Tab Bar
components.usage:
let bar = HomeTabBar(items: [
    .init(icon: UIImage(systemName: "house"), title: "Home") { print("home") },
    .init(icon: UIImage(systemName: "magnifyingglass"), title: "Search") { print("search") },
    .init(icon: UIImage(systemName: "person"), title: "You") { print("you") },
])
bar.onChange = { print("tab", $0) }
view.addSubview(bar)

variants.description: A pill slides behind the active tab. The default.
variants.title: Pill

variants.description: An underline slides under the active tab.
variants.title: Underline

variants.description: A dot marks the active tab.
variants.title: Dot

components.description: A generic horizontal list — pass items and a cell builder. Paging, snapping, peek (a sliver of the next item), and optional scale/alpha falloff for off-center items.
components.name: horizontal-scroller
components.title: Horizontal Scroller

variants.description: Snaps one full page at a time.
variants.title: Paged

variants.description: Shows a sliver of the next item.
variants.title: Peek

components.description: A tactile button with four background styles — glass, transparent, filled, soft — and three press effects: scale, touch-point ripple, and glow. Haptics included.
components.name: inlay-button
components.title: Inlay Button

variants.description: Clear background with a tinted border.
variants.title: Transparent

components.description: A looping loading indicator with three styles — rotating arc, pulsing dots, and equalizer bars. Auto-starts on screen, zero dependencies.
components.name: loading-indicator
components.title: Loading Indicator
components.usage:
let loader = LoadingIndicator()
view.addSubview(loader)
loader.translatesAutoresizingMaskIntoConstraints = false
NSLayoutConstraint.activate([
    loader.centerXAnchor.constraint(equalTo: view.centerXAnchor),
    loader.centerYAnchor.constraint(equalTo: view.centerYAnchor),
])

variants.description: A single rotating stroked arc. The default.
variants.title: Arc

components.description: An odometer where only the digits that change roll vertically to their new value, with grouping separators, prefix/suffix, fraction digits, and a per-column cascade. Great for counters and prices.
components.name: number-roller
components.title: Number Roller

components.description: One-time passcode entry with auto-advancing boxes, SMS autofill, paste support, a glowing focused box, digit pop-in, and a shake-on-error animation.
components.name: otp-code-input
components.title: OTP Code Input

components.description: A swipeable image carousel — one image per page, bottom-center page dots, and tap-to-jump paging. Fills whatever size you give it.
components.name: paged-image-view
components.title: Paged Image View

variants.description: Images fill the frame, cropping to fit. The default.
variants.title: Fill

variants.description: Images scale to fit without cropping.
variants.title: Fit

components.description: A stretchy image header for a scroll view: pulling down zooms the image, scrolling up collapses it with an optional blur, a legibility gradient, and a fading title. Zero-wiring via KVO. Zero dependencies.
components.name: parallax-header
components.title: Parallax Header
components.usage:
let header = ParallaxHeader(image: UIImage(named: "cover"))
header.attach(to: scrollView)

components.description: A circular determinate progress indicator with a gradient stroke, rounded caps, a counting percentage label, an optional glowing tip, and an indeterminate spinning mode. Zero dependencies.
components.name: progress-ring
components.title: Progress Ring

variants.description: Gradient stroke from start to end color.
variants.title: Gradient

variants.description: A rotating arc for unknown progress.
variants.title: Indeterminate

components.description: A custom refresher for any scroll view whose indicator draws on proportionally to the pull (stroke circle, morphing shape, or bouncing dots), then animates while refreshing. Replaces UIRefreshControl.
components.name: pull-to-refresh
components.title: Pull to Refresh

variants.description: Draws a circle as you pull. The default.
variants.title: Stroke Circle

components.description: A tap-or-drag star rating with spring pop on fill, half-star support via a partial mask, and any SF Symbol as the symbol. Interactive or display-only.
components.name: rating-stars
components.title: Rating Stars

components.description: A rounded rectangle or circle that springs open from the center, scaling 0 to 100%. Great for success badges, expanding panels, and attention pulses.
components.name: reveal-view
components.title: Reveal View

variants.description: Rounded rectangle that scales from center. The default.
variants.title: Box

variants.description: Circle that scales from center — ideal for success badges.
variants.title: Circle

components.description: A search field that lifts and reveals a Cancel button on focus, with a debounced onTextChange. Glass or solid background, system clear button, submit and cancel callbacks.
components.name: search-bar
components.title: Search Bar

components.description: A sliding segmented control with a spring indicator (pill, underline, or solid), icon+title segments, and crossfading labels. A premium take on UISegmentedControl.
components.name: segmented-control
components.title: Segmented Control

variants.description: A pill slides behind the selection. The default.
variants.title: Pill

components.description: Swipeable tab pages with a header indicator (underline or pill) that tracks the scroll offset live, plus tap-to-page. The Twitter-profile pattern, done right.
components.name: segmented-pager
components.title: Segmented Pager

variants.description: An underline tracks the active tab. The default.
variants.title: Underline

variants.description: A pill tracks the active tab.
variants.title: Pill

components.description: A polished settings list row — SF Symbol in a rounded color tile, title with optional subtitle, and a trailing chevron, value, or switch. Springy press feedback and haptics.
components.name: settings-row
components.title: Settings Row

variants.description: Drill-in row with a trailing chevron. The default.
variants.title: Chevron

variants.description: Row with a trailing switch; use onToggle.
variants.title: Toggle

variants.description: Row showing a current value plus chevron.
variants.title: Value

components.description: A generic vertical list — pass items and a cell builder. Self-sizing rows, optional separators, inset-grouped cards, and a capped staggered fade-and-slide entrance.
components.name: vertical-list
components.title: Vertical List

components.description: A model-driven settings UI with inset-grouped cards and five row types — toggle, disclosure, value, button, slider — each with a tinted SF Symbol tile. Composite: built on vertical-list.
components.name: settings-screen
components.title: Settings Screen
components.usage:
let screen = SettingsScreen(sections: [
    .init(header: "General", rows: [
        .toggle(icon: UIImage(systemName: "moon.fill"), iconTint: .systemIndigo,
                title: "Dark Mode", isOn: true) { on in print(on) },
        .disclosure(icon: UIImage(systemName: "bell.fill"), iconTint: .systemRed,
                    title: "Notifications") { print("open") },
    ]),
])
view.addSubview(screen)  // pin to edges

components.description: A slide-in side menu with a spring, a fading backdrop, optional parallax on the dimmed background, and an interactive edge-pan to open or close. Self-contained.
components.name: side-drawer
components.title: Side Drawer
components.usage:
let drawer = SideDrawer(contentView: menuView)
drawer.present(in: view)   // drawer.toggle()

variants.description: Slides in from the leading edge. The default.
variants.title: Leading

variants.description: Slides in from the trailing edge.
variants.title: Trailing

components.description: Loading placeholders with a sweeping gradient shimmer, plus ready-made builders for placeholder text lines and list-cell cards. Zero dependencies.
components.name: skeleton-shimmer
components.title: Skeleton Shimmer

components.description: A launch animation built from a single letter — fade-and-scale, stroke-draw (writes the glyph outline), or mask-reveal. Fires onFinish so you can push the home screen.
components.name: splash-screen
components.title: Splash Screen

variants.description: The letter springs in. The default.
variants.title: Fade + Scale

variants.description: The glyph outline is drawn on stroke-by-stroke.
variants.title: Stroke Draw

variants.description: The letter is revealed via a scaling mask.
variants.title: Mask Reveal

components.description: An animated success/error badge — a ring strokes on, the checkmark or cross draws after it, and the badge springs in with a matching haptic. Perfect for confirmation overlays.
components.name: status-feedback
components.title: Status Feedback
components.usage:
let done = StatusFeedbackView()
view.addSubview(done)
done.translatesAutoresizingMaskIntoConstraints = false
NSLayoutConstraint.activate([
    done.centerXAnchor.constraint(equalTo: view.centerXAnchor),
    done.centerYAnchor.constraint(equalTo: view.centerYAnchor),
])

variants.description: Green ring + checkmark, success haptic. The default.
variants.title: Success

components.description: A value stepper whose number rolls vertically on change, with press feedback, accelerating long-press repeat, and bounds-aware fading. Pill or split layout.
components.name: stepper
components.title: Stepper

components.description: Custom swipe-to-reveal actions on either side of any content view, with spring snapping, rubber-band overscroll, and a full-swipe gesture that triggers the first action.
components.name: swipe-actions-row
components.title: Swipe Actions Row
components.usage:
let row = SwipeActionRow(contentView: cell, trailingActions: [
    .init(icon: UIImage(systemName: "trash"), title: "Delete", backgroundColor: .systemRed, tint: .white) { delete() },
])
view.addSubview(row)

variants.description: Actions on the trailing edge. The default.
variants.title: Trailing

variants.description: A full swipe triggers the first action.
variants.title: Full Swipe

components.description: A Tinder-style draggable card stack: the top card rotates with the drag, like/nope overlays track direction, cards fling past a threshold, and the peeked stack animates forward. Generic, with undo.
components.name: swipe-deck
components.title: Swipe Deck

components.description: An interactive 3D parallax tilt card with a moving specular sheen and a shadow that shifts with the tilt — trading-card feel. Driven by touch or device gyroscope.
components.name: tilt-card
components.title: Tilt Card
components.usage:
let tilt = TiltCard(contentView: posterView)
view.addSubview(tilt)

variants.description: Tilts toward the touch point. The default.
variants.title: Touch

components.description: A transient notification banner with info/success/warning/error styles, spring entrance, swipe-to-dismiss, a draining progress bar, glass or solid background, and stacking. Static show() API.
components.name: toast
components.title: Toast
components.usage:
Toast.show("Saved to your library", style: .success)
// Toast.show("Something went wrong", style: .error)

components.description: Text that types itself in with a blinking caret, plus a cycling mode that types, deletes, and retypes a list of strings — the hero-tagline effect. Zero dependencies.
components.name: typewriter-label
components.title: Typewriter Label

variants.description: Types, deletes, and retypes a list — looping.
variants.title: Cycling
